steps.m: and he walks/is walking (connected to يَأْكُلُ - he eats)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect this action with the previous verb يَأْكُلُ (he eats) in the verse
This is a basic preposition showing location or place. Here it connects with يَمْشِى (walks) to show where the walking happens.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Connected the root letters to form the basic noun for a marketplace
The word for 'market' changes to 'markets' by adding 'أ' at the start and 'ا' in the middle - this is a special Arabic way to make some nouns plural

steps.m: the markets (specific markets being referred to)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it refer to specific markets rather than any markets

steps.m: in the markets (object of يَمْشِى فِى)
steps.n: The kasra shows this word follows فِى (in) in the phrase 'walks in the markets'
This is a conditional particle that expresses hypothetical situations
Adding لا changes 'if' to express a wish or desire that something would happen. Here it questions why the messenger eats (يَأْكُلُ) like regular people.

Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb

Added sukun before attaching the pronoun

steps.m: to him (connected to أُنزِلَ - was sent down)
steps.n: Added the pronoun هِ meaning 'him' at the end. In this ayah, it refers to the one to whom something was sent down.
Added fathas to create the noun pattern for beings with special powers or abilities
Added double-damma to show this is any angel, not a specific one. This angel would be the subject of يَكُونَ (be/become) in this ayah.
Added vowels to connect the root letters
Changed to present tense form with يَ prefix and ُ ending
Changed ending vowel to fatha to show subjunctive mood

steps.m: so/then he should be (result of أُنزِلَ - was sent down)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this is a result of the previous action (an angel being sent down)
This is a preposition indicating accompaniment. Added fatha marks to show correct pronunciation.

This is a connecting word that gives two options in this ayah: either 'a treasure is thrown to him' or 'he has a garden'. It's like saying 'either this or that'.

steps.m: to throw, cast, or deliver something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense

steps.m: is being thrown/cast (to إِلَيْهِ - to him)

someone who does wrong or commits crimes

Added َ at the end because these criminals are being acted upon by the word جَعَلْنَا (we made/appointed)

steps.m: and he/it was sufficient (subject of بِرَبِّكَ)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this word with what came before it. The small alif (ٰ) is added to show the long vowel sound.

steps.m: to nurture, care for, and sustain completely

steps.n: The doubled middle letter (shadda) shows this is someone who does this action completely and perfectly
Added كَ at end to show this belongs to the person being spoken to (you)

steps.m: by your Lord (used as an oath)
steps.n: Added بِ at start to make this into an oath, like saying 'I swear by your Lord'

steps.m: to guide or show the way

steps.n: Changed from root letters to past tense verb 'he guided' by adding vowel marks

steps.m: one who guides (acted on by كَفَىٰ)
steps.n: Changed to active participle form meaning 'one who guides'. This word is affected by the verb كَفَىٰ (is sufficient)
Added tanwin alif to show this is indefinite (like saying 'a guide' instead of 'the guide') and is being acted upon by the verb كَفَىٰ (is sufficient)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Changes from a verb (he helped) to a noun meaning someone who helps a lot
Makes the word indefinite and shows it's being used as an object

steps.m: a supporter/defender (as the object being described)
steps.n: Added 'wa' (and) at the beginning to connect it to what came before. The word means 'a helper/supporter' and has tanwin (double marks) to show it's receiving the action.

steps.m: and a supporter/defender (as the object being described)

steps.n: Adding vowel marks to make basic past tense verb

This is a conditional particle that introduces hypothetical situations

The word changed to show something was done to it rather than it doing the action

steps.m: all at once (describes how القرءان was sent down)

Added tanween (ً) to show this word is describing something

steps.m: one/single (feminine, as a description of القرءان)

Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense form

Added plural marker وا to show multiple people

steps.m: to see or perceive with eyes or mind

Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. The و appears because of the weak letter pattern

steps.m: they see it (referring to the town mentioned in the ayah)
steps.n: Added ها to show what is being seen (referring back to the town القرية mentioned earlier in the verse)

steps.m: to exist or be in a state

steps.n: Added fathas to show this happened in the past. The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a special type of word (called a hollow verb).

steps.m: they were (referring to the people of the town mentioned in ٱلْقَرْيَةِ)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to change 'he' to 'they' (talking about multiple people)
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يَرْجُونَ (they hope) into 'they do not hope'. It's one of the most basic ways to say 'no' or make something negative in Arabic.
Added a fatha mark (َ) on the ل to show how it's pronounced

steps.m: to hope for or expect something

steps.n: Added يَ at the start to show 'he' in present tense, and vowels to make it a complete word meaning 'he hopes'
Added ن at the end to show that multiple people are doing the action

steps.m: to spread out or bring back to life

steps.n: Added vowels ُ (u) and turned the root into a noun meaning 'rising from death'

steps.m: resurrection (as the object being hoped for)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object of يَرْجُونَ (they hope for) in the ayah
This is a conditional particle that introduces a time condition

steps.m: and when (introduces a time condition in رَأَوْكَ - when they see you)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before it. This word introduces the time condition 'when they see you' in the ayah.

steps.m: to see/perceive with eyes or mind

steps.n: Added fatha marks to connect the root letters and show this happened in the past
Changed ى to وْ to show a group of people did the seeing

steps.m: they saw you (object of إِن يَتَّخِذُونَكَ)
steps.n: Added كَ to show you are the one being seen. This word connects to إِن يَتَّخِذُونَكَ (indeed they take you) in the ayah, showing how the disbelievers viewed the Prophet ﷺ.
This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition. In this ayah, it connects with the verb يَتَّخِذُونَكَ (they take you) to mean 'if/when they take you'

steps.m: to adopt, to take for oneself
steps.n: Added ا at beginning and doubled ت after first root letter to show doing something to/for oneself
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action

steps.m: they take you (as mockery - object of رَأَوْكَ)
steps.n: Added كَ to show 'you' as the one being taken. This word appears in a verse where the disbelievers are mocking the Prophet, taking him as an object of ridicule when they see him.
This is a basic word that shows something is being left out or excluded from what was mentioned before. It's like saying 'but not' in English.
The shadda and alif are added to make the word stronger and clearer. In this ayah, it shows that the people are doing nothing with the Prophet (peace be upon him) except making fun.
Changed the verb into a noun by adding damma marks

steps.m: as a mockery (object of يَتَّخِذُونَكَ - they take you)
steps.n: Added و and tanween alif to show this is the object being acted upon in the sentence. This word describes how 'they take you' (يَتَّخِذُونَكَ).
This is a demonstrative pronoun that points to something near

steps.m: is this? (asking mockingly about رَسُولًا - messenger)
steps.n: The أَ at the start turns this into a question. In this ayah, it's used by disbelievers to mock the idea of someone being Allah's messenger.

steps.m: the one who (refers to someone they're mocking in أَهٰذَا)
steps.n: Added ال at the beginning which combines with ذِى to make a relative pronoun. The ل gets a shadda because it blends with the following letter. This word connects back to أَهٰذَا (is this the one) in the question they're asking mockingly.

steps.m: to send forth, to raise up, to resurrect

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the past tense verb. This word is connected to رَسُولًا (messenger) in this ayah, showing who Allah sent.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel marks
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject
Connected the root letters together with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changes from a verb (sent) to a noun (messenger) by adding و and changing vowels
Added tanween alif to show this is the object being acted upon by the verb بَعَثَ (sent/appointed)
This is a conditional particle that shows the next part depends on something. Here it introduces a condition about 'he almost led us astray'.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this is past tense

steps.m: he almost did (subject of لَيُضِلُّنَا - to lead us astray)
steps.n: The middle letter و changed to ا because it's in the middle of the word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Adding vowels makes it a past tense verb
Adding يُ at the start and doubling the middle letter changes this from 'going astray' to 'making others go astray'

steps.m: in order to make (someone) lose the way
steps.n: Adding لِ shows this is happening for a reason or purpose

steps.m: he will surely lead us astray (affected by كَادَ)
steps.n: Two changes: 1) لِ changed to لَ to mean 'surely/definitely', 2) نَا added at end to show 'us' as the ones being led astray. Comes after كَادَ (was about to) in the verse.
This is a preposition that shows movement away from something or talking about something. In this ayah, it's used with ءَالِهَتِنَا (our gods) to show they were trying to lead people away from them.
Forms the basic noun for 'god' by adding vowels to connect the letters
Adds the feminine marker ة to change 'god' to 'goddess'

steps.m: our goddesses (object of يُضِلُّنَا - to lead us astray)
steps.n: Adds 'our' (نا) at the end to show these are 'our goddesses'. Also changes the word slightly to match Arabic grammar rules when adding 'our'.
This is a basic conditional particle made of two parts: لو (if) and لا (not). When combined, they create a special meaning showing what would have happened if something hadn't prevented it.

steps.m: if not for (connecting to أَن صَبَرْنَا - that we were patient)
steps.n: The ا is lengthened to آ for emphasis. This word connects to the phrase after it (أَن صَبَرْنَا) to show what might have happened if they hadn't been patient.

steps.m: that (connects to صَبَرْنَا - we were patient)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that introduces what happened - in this case, it connects to the next word صَبَرْنَا (we were patient). It works like saying 'that we were patient' in English.

steps.m: to be patient or persevere through difficulty

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action. This word is part of a response to لَوْلَآ (if not for) in this ayah.
This is a preposition that shows something is physically or metaphorically above something else
The word changes shape to prepare for adding a pronoun

steps.m: upon her/it (object of صَبَرْنَا - we remained patient)
steps.n: Added ها which means 'her' or 'it' when referring to feminine things. This shows what they remained patient upon.

steps.m: will (definitely in the future, connected to يَعْلَمُونَ - they will know)
steps.n: This word shows something will definitely happen in the future. It's stronger than just 'will'. Here it connects to the verb يَعْلَمُونَ (they will know) to emphasize they will definitely come to know.

steps.m: and will definitely (connected to يَعْلَمُونَ - they will know)
steps.n: وَ at the start means 'and', connecting this statement to what came before. The full phrase وَسَوْفَ يَعْلَمُونَ means 'and they will definitely come to know' - showing that despite their current denial, they cannot escape the future realization.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

steps.m: they know (referring to the disbelievers in the ayah)
steps.n: The ون ending shows that multiple people are doing the action. In this ayah, it refers to the disbelievers who will know when they see the punishment.
Adding the kasra vowel helps make this a noun meaning 'time'
Adding the fatha at the end shows this word is being used as a direct object
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added يَ at start to show present tense. The أ and ي merge into a long ى, which is common with this type of verb

steps.m: they see (subject of يَعْلَمُونَ - they will know)
steps.n: Added ون at the end to show multiple people doing the action. The و appears because it comes from the original root. This word is connected to يَعْلَمُونَ (they will know) in the ayah.

steps.m: to punish severely or cause pain

Added ٱلْ (the) at the start to make it specific and َ at the end to show it's receiving the action of seeing

Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
The double letter shows emphasis and makes it easier to say

steps.m: more lost from the right path
steps.n: Added أَ at the beginning and ُ on the end to show comparison (more/most). This word appears in a context comparing different levels of being lost from guidance.

steps.m: Did you see? (asking about مَنِ ٱتَّخَذَ)

steps.m: to grasp or take hold of something

steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this is the object being acted upon. In this ayah, it shows what they are more astray in - they are more astray in path/way.

steps.m: to see or perceive with eyes or mind

Added ت at the beginning to change from 'he' to 'you', and the hamza (ء) drops because it's weak between vowels

steps.m: you see (addressing the Prophet ﷺ in أَلَمْ تَرَ - 'Do you not see?')

This is a basic preposition showing direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects to رَبِّكَ (your Lord) to show 'to your Lord'

steps.m: to care for, nurture, develop completely

Adding kasra shows this word is being connected to something else, like saying 'my Lord' or 'Lord of'

steps.m: how (asking about the way مَدَّ happens)

Adding the fatha at the end shows this is a complete question word
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters

Added kasra and shadda to make it a noun meaning the thing itself (shadow) rather than the action of casting a shadow
Added ال to specify this particular shadow (the one being extended by Allah in this ayah)
Added fatha at the end because this shadow is the object being extended (مَدَّ) by Allah in this ayah
Added vowel marks to show how to pronounce this conditional word. This word connects to شَآءَ (He willed) to express 'if He willed'
Added و at the beginning to connect this condition to the previous statement

steps.m: to want or wish for something

steps.n: Added fathas to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he wanted'
Changed to a hollow verb pattern by adding long alif

steps.m: to make or set something into a particular state

steps.m: he would surely have made (conditional response)

steps.m: he would surely have made it (referring to الظِّلَّ - the shadow)
steps.n: The هُ suffix shows what is being made/set (the shadow). The small ۥ mark is just a pronunciation guide.
Added ا after first letter and ِ before last letter to show who/what has this quality

steps.m: still/motionless (describing how Allah could have made الظِّلَّ/the shadow)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word is describing how something was made. Here it's describing how Allah could have made the shadow.

Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed the last vowel to a sukun to prepare for adding نا
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' are doing the action. This word appears in the ayah after لَ which means 'surely', showing emphasis.
Connected the root letters together with vowel marks to make it a proper word
Added 'the' to make it specific. This word is being acted upon by جَعَلْنَا (We made/placed)
Added the fatha mark at the end to show this is the object being acted upon by جَعَلْنَا (We made/placed)

steps.m: upon it/him (referring to الظِّلَّ - the shadow)
steps.n: Added هِ (him/it) to show what is being referred to. In this ayah, it refers back to الظِّلَّ (the shadow) mentioned earlier.

steps.m: to guide or show the way

Changed the pattern to show someone or something that performs the action of guiding
Added tanween fatHa to show this is the object being acted upon by جَعَلْنَا (we made) in this ayah
This is the basic form of the word that shows something happens after something else. It has no root because it's a connecting word.

steps.m: then (connects to قَبَضْنٰهُ - we drew/seized it)
steps.n: Added vowel marks and shadda to complete the word. In this ayah, it shows that the seizing/drawing happens after something mentioned before.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added sukun to prepare for attaching pronouns
Added نا to show 'we' did the action
Added هُ to show what was seized. This connects to إِلَيْنَا (to Us) in the ayah, showing Allah seizing something to Himself.
Basic preposition showing direction or destination. This word has no root since it's a particle.
The ى changes to ي before adding pronouns

steps.m: to/towards us (object of قَبَضْنٰهُ/we drew it)

Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create a noun form
Added tanwin alif to show this is the object being acted upon. This word explains what type of seizing was done in قَبَضْنٰهُ (we seized it).
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters
Added ي between second and third root letters to intensify the meaning
Added tanween alif to show this word is receiving the action in the sentence
Added vowel marks to show the correct pronunciation of the basic pronoun
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it. This pronoun 'he' refers to Allah who is the subject of the verb جَعَلَ (made/placed) in this ayah.
This is the base form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to people or things. Here it connects to the verb جَعَلَ (made/placed) to tell us who did the action.

steps.m: The one who (connects to جَعَلَ - made)
steps.n: Added ٱلّ at the beginning to make it specific. The shadda appears because ذ is a 'sun letter' that combines with the ل of ٱل.

steps.m: to make or set something as something else

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word is part of describing what Allah did - making the night as a covering.

Changed to the unique name of the One True God by adding ٱل, doubling the ل with shadda, and adding ِ at the end to show this word is connected to what comes after it in the ayah

steps.m: to return back (to the right way)

steps.n: Adds مَ at start to show where/how the returning happens
Adding ًا shows this describes how the returning/repenting happens in the phrase 'returns to Allah with [this type of] return' (يَتُوبُ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ مَتَابًا)
Added ن to show we're talking about many people, not just one person
Added ال at the beginning to make it point to specific people. The ّ (shadda) shows emphasis on the letter ذ

steps.m: and those who (subject of لَا يَشْهَدُونَ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this with what came before. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb لَا يَشْهَدُونَ (they do not witness) in this ayah.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يَشْهَدُونَ (they witness) negative, so it means 'they do not witness'
Added fatha mark to show the correct pronunciation

steps.m: to witness/testify to something you saw or know

steps.n: Adding vowel marks to make it a complete past tense verb
Added يَ at start and changed vowels to show this happens in the present/future
Added ونَ at the end to show multiple people are doing the action
Added damma (ُ) on first letter and made middle letter و long to create a noun meaning the result of lying/turning from truth
Added ٱل (the) at start and doubled ز with shadda to show it's a specific falsehood being talked about

steps.m: the falsehood (object of يَشْهَدُونَ - they witness)
steps.n: Added fatha (َ) at the end to show this word is receiving the action of the verb يَشْهَدُونَ (they witness/testify). The verse is saying they do not witness/testify to falsehood.
This is a conditional particle that introduces a time condition, similar to saying 'when' or 'whenever' in English. In this ayah, it introduces what these people do when they encounter idle talk.
Adds و at the beginning to connect this phrase with the previous statement about not bearing false witness. It shows these are related good qualities of the same people.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
The middle letter is doubled to show the action was done with more intensity

steps.m: they passed by (subject of the verb)
steps.n: Added وا to show multiple people did the action
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ل because it comes after ال

steps.m: with the idle talk (object of مَرُّوا۟ - they pass by)
steps.n: Added بِ at start to mean 'with' and ِ at end because it follows the preposition. The word shows what they pass by in the ayah.
Root letters م-ر-ر got vowel marks (َ) to make 'he passed by' in past tense
Combined the doubled letter with shadda for easier pronunciation
Added وا to show many people did the action (when they passed by idle talk/vain speech)
Added the vowels fatHa (َ) and Damma (ُ) to make it mean 'he was honorable' in the past
Changes from 'he was honorable' to 'honorable ones' by using broken plural pattern
Added tanween alif to make it describe 'how' they passed by (like saying 'they passed by in an honorable way')
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that refers to someone or something
Adding ن at the end makes it refer to multiple people
Adding ال makes it mean 'the ones who' or 'those who'. The ّ (shadda) shows emphasis on the ل
Adding و at the beginning connects this to what came before it, like saying 'and'

steps.m: when (followed by ذُكِّرُوا - they are reminded)
steps.n: This is a time word that introduces something that happens repeatedly or is expected to happen. In this ayah, it introduces what these people do when they are reminded of Allah's signs.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added damma at start and doubled the middle letter to show something was done to the subject
Added وا to show multiple people were reminded. This word relates to the signs (آيات) of their Lord mentioned after it.

steps.m: to show, make clear, or demonstrate

steps.n: Changes from a root meaning 'show' into a noun meaning 'a sign' by adding long aa sound at start
Changes from one sign to multiple signs
Adds بِ to show these signs are being used or dealt with in some way

steps.m: with/by the signs (of رَبِّهِمْ - their Lord)
steps.n: Adds ِ to show these signs belong to their Lord (رَبِّهِمْ) in this ayah

steps.m: to care for and help grow
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make them readable

steps.m: one who cares for and helps others grow completely
steps.n: The doubled middle letter (shadda) shows this person does a lot of caring and helping others grow

steps.m: one who cares for and helps others grow completely
steps.n: Added kasra to prepare for adding the next part

steps.m: their caring Lord (who is being remembered through اللَّهِ's signs)
steps.n: Added هِمْ at the end to show this is their Lord. This word connects to the earlier part of the verse where Allah's signs are being remembered.
This is the basic negative word that shows something didn't happen
Adding مْ makes it specifically about things that didn't happen in the past. This word makes the verb يَخِرُّوا۟ (they fall) into past tense.
Added vowels to make 'he fell down' in past tense - like adding -ed in English
Added shadda to show intensity of the action
Added يَ at start and changed vowel to show present tense
Added plural masculine ending to show multiple people doing the action
This is a basic preposition that shows something is on top of or upon something else
The ى changes to يْ to prepare for adding a pronoun suffix

steps.m: upon it/them (referring to the signs/verses of their Lord mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added ها at the end to show what is being acted upon. Here it refers back to the signs/verses (آيات) mentioned earlier in the ayah.

steps.m: to block or close up (especially about hearing)

steps.n: Root means 'block/close' - doubled middle letter makes it mean 'deaf' (someone whose hearing is blocked)
Changed vowel to make plural form
Added an alif at the end to show these deaf people are being described or talked about (they are the object in the sentence)
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changes to plural form for a group of blind people
Added tanween fatha to show this word is being acted upon in the sentence
Added و at the beginning to connect this word with what came before it
Added ين to show we're talking about multiple people
Added ال to point to specific people being talked about
Added و at the beginning to connect this to what came before. This word introduces people who are saying something (يَقُولُونَ means 'they say')
The middle weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change with this type of word
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action

steps.m: to care for and develop something to its full potential
steps.n: The word رب means 'lord' or 'master'
Connected the letters and added shadda to show intensity, creating the noun for the one who does this caring and nurturing completely

steps.m: our Lord (object of يَقُولُونَ - they say)
steps.n: Added نَا at the end to show this is 'our Lord'. In this ayah, this is what the people are saying (the object of يَقُولُونَ - they say)

steps.m: to give freely as a gift

steps.n: Added vowels to make it a past tense verb

steps.m: give! (as a gift) (command to Allah in رَبَّنَا هَبْ)
steps.n: Changed to command form by removing initial و and adding sukun at end. This is a special command form used when making a request to Allah in the context of prayer.
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added نا which means 'us' to show who the action is for. This word connects to هَبْ (grant) in the ayah, showing what is being requested.
This is a preposition that shows where something comes from or what it's made of. In this ayah, it connects to the word أَزْوٰجِنَا (our spouses) to show the source.
Connected the root letters together with fatha and sukun to form the basic noun meaning one spouse or pair
Added أَ at the beginning and ا after و to form the broken plural pattern
Added kasra at the end because this word comes after the preposition مِنْ (from)
Added نَا at the end to show possession - these are our spouses

steps.m: to spread out/scatter (like seeds or descendants)
steps.n: Forms the word for 'offspring' or 'descendants'
Creates a noun meaning 'offspring' by adding ي and ة
Makes the word plural by adding the feminine plural ending ات
Adds kasra at the end to show it's being acted upon by something
Forms a noun meaning 'coolness' or 'comfort'
Adding ة at the end makes it a concrete noun
Added fatha at the end because this word is the object of هَبْ (grant) in the phrase 'grant us... comfort of our eyes'
Added vowel marks to form the basic singular noun for 'eye'
Changed from singular 'eye' to plural 'eyes' by adding أَ at the start and changing middle vowels - a common Arabic plural pattern
Added tanween (two dots under) to show this word connects with قُرَّةَ, meaning 'comfort of eyes'
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he made' to 'make!' by adding alif at start and putting sukoon at end - this makes it a command
Added نا at the end to show who is being made/appointed
Added و at beginning to connect this command with previous phrases in the ayah
Connected the root letters together
Added مُ at start to show who does the action
The و changed to ت and merged with another ت, showing someone who protects themselves by being careful about their actions towards God
Added ين to make it plural (more than two)
Added لِلْ (لِ + الْ) at beginning to mean 'for the'
Changes the root into a noun meaning someone who leads. The إِ at the start and ا after م show this is someone who does the action.

steps.m: a leader (object of وَٱجْعَلْنَا - make us)
steps.n: Adds tanween alif to show this word is receiving the action of the verb وَٱجْعَلْنَا (make us). The full meaning in context is 'make us leaders'.
This is the base form of the demonstrative pronoun used to point to multiple people or things
The ٓ (madd) shows that we're pointing to something far away

steps.m: those people over there (subject of يُجْزَوْنَ)
steps.n: The ئِكَ ending makes this word specifically point to a group being discussed. This word is the subject of يُجْزَوْنَ (they are rewarded) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to passive voice present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: they are rewarded (referring to the dwellers of Paradise)
steps.n: Changed to plural by adding وْنَ at the end. The ى changes to و because it's a weak letter meeting the plural ending
Changed from a verb meaning 'to scoop' into a noun meaning 'high room' by adding vowels
Added ة to specify a single chamber
Added ال to make it specific
Added fatha at the end because this word is the object of the verb يُجْزَوْنَ (they are rewarded with)
This is a relative pronoun that refers to something general or abstract

steps.m: because of what (they did in صَبَرُوا)
steps.n: Added the preposition بِ which shows cause/reason here. This word connects to صَبَرُوا (they were patient) to explain why they get the reward mentioned.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: they endured patiently (those who will be rewarded with يُجْزَوْنَ)
steps.n: Added وا to show many people did this action. This refers to the believers mentioned earlier who will be rewarded with high places in Paradise.
Added shadda to show intensive meaning of receiving
Changed to passive form to show being received/greeted
Added plural masculine ending to show multiple people
Added conjunction و to connect with previous phrase
Starting with the basic preposition في meaning 'in'

steps.m: in it (feminine) (referring to الْغُرْفَةَ - the lofty chamber)
steps.n: Added ها at the end to show this refers to something feminine (the lofty chamber) mentioned earlier in the ayah
Connected root letters with vowels to form basic past tense verb
Added تَ prefix and doubled ي to change from 'living' to 'giving greetings/salutations'
Added ة to show one specific instance of greeting
Added ً to show this is what they will receive in Paradise, connecting to يُلَقَّوْنَ (they will be met with) in the ayah

steps.m: to be safe and at peace

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to be safe' into the noun 'peace'
Added ًا at the end to show this peace is part of the greeting mentioned in the ayah

steps.m: and peace (as part of a greeting)
steps.n: Added conjunction و meaning 'and'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Adding alif after first root letter makes it describe someone who does the action. This word is in accusative case as it describes a state.

steps.m: those who stay/remain (describing the state of مُسْتَقَرًّا وَمُقَامًا)
steps.n: Changed from singular (one person) to plural (many people) by adding ين at the end
Added final vowel mark to show this describes a state. The word connects to فِيهَا which tells where they are dwelling.
This is a basic preposition showing location or being inside something
Added kasra vowel mark to show correct pronunciation

steps.m: in it (f) (referring to Paradise جَنَّة mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added ها which refers back to Paradise (جَنَّة) which is feminine in Arabic. This shows who or what is being referred to.
This pattern shows something has a natural quality

steps.m: it (Paradise) has become beautiful (as a resting place)
steps.n: Added ت to show this is describing Paradise (which is feminine in Arabic). This word connects to مُسْتَقَرًّا (resting place) that comes after it in the ayah.

steps.m: to settle or remain in one place

steps.n: Doubling the ر makes the meaning stronger - really settled down
Added مُسْتَ at the beginning to show this is a place where the action happens

steps.m: a place of settling (object of حَسُنَتْ)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this is the object being described as good by the verb حَسُنَتْ (it became good)

steps.m: to stand or stay in a place

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past-tense verb
Added مُ at the beginning to change from the action of staying to the place where one stays
Added ًا to show this is being acted upon by the verb حَسُنَتْ (how excellent)

steps.m: and a dwelling place (object of حَسُنَتْ)
steps.n: Added وَ to connect this word with مُسْتَقَرًّا (place of rest) from the previous part of the verse
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense form
Changed to command form by adding damma at start and sukun at end. The middle weak letter و drops out in this form.
In this ayah, مَا is used as a negative particle that negates the verb يَعْبَؤُا۟ (would care). The complete phrase مَا يَعْبَؤُا means 'would not care'.

steps.m: to care for or pay attention to

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the past tense verb
Changes from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowel marks
Added وا at the end to show multiple people doing the action. This verb is connected to يَكُونُ (it will be) in the ayah.
Added kasra to show this is a preposition meaning 'with' or 'by'
Added كُمْ to show this refers to multiple people being addressed. This word is the object of يَعْبَؤُا (he cares about) in this ayah.
The doubled letter shows the complete nature of lordship - having full ability to nurture and care for others
Kasra shows this word will be connected to something that comes after it

steps.m: my Lord (the one who nurtures and cares for me)
steps.n: Adding ى shows this is talking about my own Lord. In this ayah, it's being used as the Prophet ﷺ speaks about his Lord.
This is a conditional word that introduces a hypothetical scenario

steps.m: if not, were it not for (introduces condition for دُعَآؤُكُمْ)
steps.n: Adding لا changes the meaning to express something that would have happened, but didn't. In this ayah, it introduces a condition related to دُعَآؤُكُمْ (your supplication/prayer)

steps.m: to call out to someone or make a request

steps.n: Changes from 'to call' into 'a call/prayer' by adding ُ (du) at start and ا (aa) after second letter, with ء (hamza) at end
Changes the spelling of alif to alif madd, which is just a spelling variation
Adds the u-vowel at the end to show this word is the subject
Adds كُمْ to show the prayer belongs to multiple people being addressed
Added vowel marks to show this is a particle meaning 'certainly' or 'indeed'. In this ayah, it emphasizes that the denial has definitely happened.
Added فَ at the beginning to connect this statement with what comes before it. Here it connects to the word كَذَّبْتُمْ (you have denied), showing a sequence of events.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make it a past tense verb
The doubled middle letter makes the meaning stronger - showing complete or emphatic rejection
Added تُمْ to show that multiple people being spoken to (you all) did this action in the past
This is a future particle that shows something will definitely happen soon. It's stronger and more certain than just س-.

steps.m: then soon/so in the future (before يَكُونُ)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this happens as a result of what came before. It connects to the previous statement about rejection, showing there will be consequences.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense form

steps.m: he/it is/exists (part of فَسَوْفَ يَكُونُ - 'then it will be')
steps.n: Added يَ at start to show present/future tense. Changed middle vowel to ُ and added ُ at end to show it's happening now or will happen. In this ayah, it comes after فَسَوْفَ (then will) to mean 'then it will be'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed from a verb (he made necessary) to a noun (necessity) by changing vowels and adding alif at the end
Added tanween (double mark) at the end to show this word describes what something 'will be'
